steps.title: Identify the slope direction of the data
steps.content: Looking at the scatter plot, as $x$ increases, $y$ consistently increases. The relationship is positive with a moderate-to-steep slope. This immediately eliminates choices A ($y = -1.8x - 0.2$) and B ($y = -0.2x - 1.8$), both of which have negative slopes that run opposite to the data.

steps.title: Compare the slopes of the remaining choices
steps.content: We're left with C and D, both with positive slopes. Choice C has slope $0.2$, which is very shallow—the line would be nearly horizontal. Choice D has slope $1.80$, which is much steeper. The scatter plot clearly shows a steep upward trend, not a nearly flat one. Slope $1.80$ matches the observed pattern far better than slope $0.2$.

steps.title: Verify with a test point
steps.content:
Test choice D with the point $(5, 9)$:
$$y = 1.80(5) + 0.2 = 9.0 + 0.2 = 9.2$$
The predicted value $9.2$ matches the actual value $9$ very closely. That matches choice D — D is correct.
